'''Ian Frederick Gildart''' (born 14 October 1969) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who in the 1980s and 1990s played for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] as a {{rlp|sr}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> before assisting at [[Wigan St Patricks]] ARLFC.

==Background==
Gildart was born in [[Ince-in-Makerfield]], [[Lancashire]], England. Ian is the father of the rugby league footballer:; [[Oliver Gildart]].<ref name="guardian20170218">{{cite web |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Wigan’s Oliver Gildart finds hope in father’s World Club Challenge story |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/feb/18/wigan-oliver-gildart-father-world-club-challenge |website=The Guardian |access-date=24 October 2024 |date=18 February 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==

===Wigan===
Gildart made his first team debut for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in a [[Challenge Cup]] preliminary round tie against [[Workington Town]] in January 1987.<ref>{{cite news |title=Gildart makes Wigan debut |url=https://www.newspapers.com/image/934365857/ |work=Manchester Evening News |date=17 January 1987 |page=3 |department=Pink Final |via=[[Newspapers.com]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

Ian Gildart was a non-playing [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in Wigan's 8–2 victory over [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[1987 World Club Challenge]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Wednesday 7 October 1987,<ref name="guardian20170218" /> and a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in the 21–4 victory over [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[1991 World Club Challenge]] at [[Anfield]], [[Liverpool]] on Wednesday 2 October 1991.

During Ian Gildart's time, there were Wigan's victories in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]] during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]], [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]], [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]], [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] and [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]].

Gildart was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s 36–14 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1990 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1989–90 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 28 April 1990.

Gildart played at {{rlp|sr}} in Wigan's 24–12 victory over [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[1989–90 League Cup (rugby league)|1989–90 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 13 January 1990.<ref name="13th January 1990: Wigan 24 Halifax 12 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2840|title=13th January 1990: Wigan 24 Halifax 12 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=14 July 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714164411/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2840|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 1994, Gildart signed a one-year contract with [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sporting Digest: Rugby League |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/sporting-digest-rugby-league-1374311.html |website=The Independent |access-date=24 October 2024 |date=3 August 1994}}</ref>
